lead_signal.headline: V-Dem reports unprecedented US democratic decline
lead_signal.summary: V-Dem Dataset v16 (through 2025) records USA LDI decline of 0.18 (24% drop), fastest in modern history, with world rank falling from 20th to 51st. Administration undercuts checks and balances, politicizes civil service, intimidates judiciary, attacks press. Active autocratisation ERT flagged.

erosion_developments.title: Unprecedented LDI decline and institutional capture
erosion_developments.summary: V-Dem reports USA Liberal Democracy Index fell 24% in one year, ranking dropping to 51/179. Second Trump term features power concentration in presidency, politicization of civil service and oversight, judicial intimidation, press attacks. Rule of law deteriorating per V-Dem v16 (end-2025 data).[1]

sources_cited.title: Democratic Backsliding Reaches Western Democracies with US Decline Unprecedented

erosion_developments.title: Ongoing erosion via immigration enforcement overreach
erosion_developments.summary: Reports detail ICE arrest surges to 800+/day, detention expansion to 70,000+, warrantless home entries violating Fourth Amendment. Daily quotas drive raids, eroding due process and public trust amid rising political violence post-Jan 6.[2]

cross_monitor_signals.scem: US political violence and institutional strain (post-Jan 6 patterns) signal state fragility risks.[2]

weekly_brief_narrative: V-Dem Institute released its 2026 Democracy Report press release detailing accelerated democratic backsliding in established Western democracies, with the USA experiencing the fastest deterioration in modern history. The US Liberal Democracy Index (LDI) declined by 0.18 points (24%) from 2024 to 2025, dropping world rank from 20th to 51st out of 179 countries; active autocratisation ERT flagged per v16 dataset (covering through end-2025).[1] Institutional effects center on executive overreach in Donald Trump's second term: rapid presidential power concentration via undercutting checks and balances, politicization of civil service and oversight bodies, intimidation of the judiciary, alongside attacks on press, academia, and civil liberties. Rule of law deteriorated in 22 countries including USA; freedom of expression shows global drastic decline, targeted by autocratizing leaders. This pattern signifies backsliding reaching traditionally stable democracies (USA, Italy, UK), with six of ten new autocratizing countries in Europe/North America; aligns with Dimension A calibration anchor of 0.57 LDI, collapse-speed trajectory score 2.5.[1] Adjacent signals include deepening immigration enforcement eroding due process—ICE daily arrests >800, detention >70,000 by Jan 2026, warrantless entries violating Fourth Amendment, quotas driving raids—compounding post-Jan 6 political violence (1,583 arrests, 1,270 convictions).[2] V-Dem notes media freedom as primary target, corroborating Tier 2 ERT status; no Tier 1 OSCE/OHCHR weekly events detected in results, but lag note applies: v16 reflects 2025 conditions, weight 2026 intelligence for current scoring. European signals (Italy/Slovakia ERT new) warrant gap audit if untracked. No electoral or export items this week; USA trajectory worsening per calibration.
